glossary.definition: A class of feedforward artificial neural network consisting of at least three layers of nodes: an input layer, a hidden layer, and an output layer, where each node except the inputs uses a non-linear activation function.
A supervised learning algorithm for training artificial neural networks that calculates the gradient of the loss function with respect to the network's weights using the chain rule of calculus.
A weight initialization method designed to keep the variance of the activations and gradients constant across layers by drawing weights from a distribution scaled by the input and output dimensions.
A layer optimization technique that standardizes the inputs to a layer for each mini-batch, accelerating training by reducing internal covariate shift.

references.title: Deep Learning
